docs.name: Chromatic Aberration Reveal
docs.description: Reveal an image with an RGB split that converges into a clean final frame. Three channel-shifted layers spring to alignment with subtle staggering, plus a one-pass wave sweep during convergence. Supports in-view once, mount-triggered, and manual visibility control.

props.name: src
props.description: Image URL rendered in the base and channel layers.

props.name: alt
props.description: Accessible alt text for the base image layer.

props.name: splitDistance
props.description: Initial horizontal channel offset in pixels (R = -distance, B = +distance).

props.name: staggerMs
props.description: Delay between each channel settling, in milliseconds.

props.name: trigger
props.description: Animation trigger mode. `in-view` runs once when entering viewport.

props.name: isVisible
props.description: Used with `trigger="manual"` to control reveal state externally.

docs.overview: ChromaticAberrationReveal stacks a base `` plus three RGB-biased overlay layers using `mix-blend-mode: screen` over a black wrapper. On reveal, red starts at `-splitDistance`, green at `0`, blue at `+splitDistance`, each at partial opacity, then they spring back to perfect alignment with staggered delays so convergence reads intentional and cinematic. A soft diagonal wave sweep runs once during convergence. `trigger="in-view"` uses IntersectionObserver with once semantics to avoid repeated replays; `trigger="manual"` enables external timeline control. Reduced-motion users get a plain fade treatment without channel split.

scenarios.title: Hero image entrance
scenarios.description: Use in-view mode to reveal a hero visual as it enters the viewport, then keep it stable.

scenarios.title: Gallery card with tighter split
scenarios.description: Lower splitDistance for a more restrained chromatic cue in dense grids.

scenarios.title: Manual reveal in controlled sequence
scenarios.description: Tie visibility to your own state machine or parent timeline.
